title: tetrazygia urbaniana
intro: Tetrazygia urbaniana, also known as the urbaniana tetrazygia or locally referred to as the "wild coffee" due to its coffee-like smell when its leaves are crushed, is a plant species belonging to the Melastomataceae family. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. Studies have shown that Tetrazygia urbaniana may provide various health benefits, such as reducing oxidative stress, improving cardiovascular health, and exhibiting antimicrobial activities. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of the plant are typically used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and digestive issues.
